branches.condition: If the /api/novu Bridge Endpoint is not publicly accessible after deployment
branches.effect_on_first_success: Blocks notification delivery from Novu Cloud to local workflow, delaying first success
branches.path: Deployment fails or requires additional network configuration

candidate_paths.name: Python SDK Quickstart
candidate_paths.relationship_to_primary_path: Alternative SDK-based integration path using Python instead of Express.js framework.
candidate_paths.selection_basis: Official Python SDK documentation provides code snippets to trigger notifications using Novu API.

steps.action: Create Novu account and obtain API key from dashboard settings
details: Access https://web.novu.co/settings to get API key

steps.action: Install the Novu Python package via pip or poetry
details: Run pip install novu or poetry add novu

steps.action: Write Python code to trigger a notification event using Novu SDK
details: Use EventApi.trigger with workflow ID and subscriber ID

steps.action: Run the Python script to trigger the notification event
details: Execute script and observe notification delivery

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Notification workflow triggered and notification sent to subscriber
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Test workflow notification is received by the subscriber as triggered from the Express.js application
documented_first_success.official_milestone: Successfully send a test workflow notification through the Novu Bridge Endpoint in the Express.js app
documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The documentation explicitly marks 'Test your endpoint' as the step confirming the workflow is running and notifications are sent, marking first successful integration.

assumed_prior_state: Developer has an existing Express.js application
Developer has basic knowledge of Node.js and Express
Developer has access to Novu dashboard to create workflows and obtain API keys
entry_point.developer_intent: Integrate Novu notification workflows into an existing Express.js application to send notifications.

friction_gates.description: Developer must create a Novu account to access dashboard and API keys.
Developer must obtain API key from Novu dashboard settings to authenticate API calls.
Developer must configure the Novu Bridge Endpoint correctly in the Express.js app and run npx novu dev with correct port.
The /api/novu endpoint must be accessible from the internet for Novu Cloud to communicate with the local workflow.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Novu account and access the Novu dashboard
Obtain an API key from the Novu dashboard settings
Have an existing Express.js application and Node.js environment set up
Create a Novu workflow definition in the application code under novu/workflows.ts

primary_path.action: Read the Express.js Quickstart guide to understand integration steps
details: Understand the need for a Bridge Endpoint
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Confusion or lack of clarity on steps
primary_path.output: Plan for integration and workflow creation

primary_path.action: Install Novu packages in the Express.js application
details: Run npm install commands as per guide
primary_path.input: Command line with npm or yarn

primary_path.action: Create workflow definition file novu/workflows.ts with desired notification workflows
details: Define workflows in code as per guide
primary_path.input: Code editor with access to project files

primary_path.action: Start the Express.js application with Novu Bridge Endpoint configured
details: Run Express server on default or specified port
Run npx novu dev with correct port if needed
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Server or Novu dev command errors
primary_path.input: Terminal commands to start server and Novu dev
primary_path.output: Express server running with Novu Bridge Endpoint
primary_path.success_signal: Server starts without errors and Bridge Endpoint is active

primary_path.action: Test the Novu Bridge Endpoint by triggering a test workflow notification
details: Send test event to Novu endpoint
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No notification received or errors in logs
primary_path.input: Test trigger command or HTTP request

primary_path.action: Deploy the Express.js application with the Novu Bridge Endpoint exposed to the internet
primary_path.success_signal: Public access to Bridge Endpoint confirmed

sources.title: Python Novu SDK — Novu SDK 1.11.0 documentation

alternatives_considered.name: Novu Python SDK Quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Python SDK documentation provides code snippets but lacks a full end-to-end onboarding flow including deployment and endpoint exposure.

alternatives_considered.name: Novu Platform Overview
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Platform overview explains concepts but does not provide a stepwise onboarding path.

surface.name: Novu Framework Express.js Quickstart
surface.selection_basis: The Express.js Quickstart guide provides a concrete, documented first-mile onboarding path with code and deployment instructions for developers integrating Novu workflows.

uncertainties.impact: Without a vendor-claimed time, it is unclear how quickly developers can expect to complete onboarding.
uncertainties.question: What is the typical time to first success claimed by Novu for the Express.js integration?
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: No explicit time-to-first-success or duration claim is stated in the official documentation.
